rag_rules: Preserve architecture by adding behavioral content only within the owning declaration's valid scope.
Keep type-level contracts limited to symbols visible at the component type; implementation-only paths belong only to valid implementation-scoped bindings.
When moving or attaching annex content, ensure the owner matches the referenced identifiers and does not expose subcomponent internals at a higher scope.
generic_rules: Do not change component structure, features, connections, or properties when inserting annex content.
Choose scope based on symbol visibility: interface-visible references stay with the type owner; implementation-specific references stay with the implementation owner.
Prefer the smallest ownership change that makes the referenced names valid and leaves existing model text intact.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL behavioral content must remain inside a valid `annex agree {** ... **};` block owned by the correct declaration.
Implementation-level bindings are only valid in implementation scope and only after any referenced structural elements are declared.
anti_patterns: Do not place references to subcomponent ports or implementation paths in a higher-level type-owned contract.
Do not relocate or rewrite unrelated architecture text just to attach an annex block.
irrelevant_topics: Concrete requirement logic for a specific signal mapping is downstream work, not model-fusion guidance.
Validator-driven type fixes for AGREE helper expressions are not relevant unless repair diagnostics are active.
